---
title: "Citation"
---

## How to Cite

Prudencio, D. (2025). International child sponsorship impact on the intended choice of acquiring a higher education degree: the case of rural Mexico. *Education Economics*. https://doi.org/10.1080/09645292.2025.2498639

**BibTeX:**

```bibtex
@article{prudencio2025childsponsorship,
  author  = {Prudencio, Daniel},
  title   = {International child sponsorship impact on the intended choice
             of acquiring a higher education degree: the case of rural {Mexico}},
  journal = {Education Economics},
  year    = {2025},
  doi     = {10.1080/09645292.2025.2498639},
  note    = {Published online: 17 May 2025}
}
```

---

## Key Reference

The structural model follows:

> Aakvik, A., Heckman, J. J., & Vytlacil, E. J. (2005). Estimating treatment effects for discrete outcomes when responses to treatment vary: An application to Norwegian vocational rehabilitation programs. *Journal of Econometrics*, 125(1–2), 15–51.

The subjective expectations methodology follows:

> Attanasio, O. P., & Kaufmann, K. M. (2014). Education choices and returns to schooling: Mothers' and youths' subjective expectations and their role in education decisions. *Journal of Development Economics*, 109, 203–216.

---

## Methodology Code

The model was estimated in **R** using maximum likelihood with Gauss-Hermite quadrature.

### Core estimation loop (simplified)

```r
library(fastGHQuad)

# Gauss-Hermite quadrature nodes and weights (K = 10)
gh   <- gaussHermiteData(10)
nodes   <- gh$x * sqrt(2)   # rescale for N(0,1)
weights <- gh$w / sqrt(pi)

# Log-likelihood for one observation
ll_i <- function(pars, S, Y, X, Z) {
  gamma <- pars[1:ncol(Z)]
  beta1 <- pars[(ncol(Z)+1):(ncol(Z)+ncol(X))]
  beta0 <- pars[(ncol(Z)+ncol(X)+1):(ncol(Z)+2*ncol(X))]
  a1    <- pars[length(pars)-1]
  a0    <- pars[length(pars)]

  # Integrate over theta using Gauss-Hermite quadrature
  integrand <- function(theta) {
    pS <- pnorm(Z %*% gamma + theta)
    pY1 <- pnorm(X %*% beta1 + a1 * theta)
    pY0 <- pnorm(X %*% beta0 + a0 * theta)

    pS^S * (1 - pS)^(1 - S) *
      (pY1^Y * (1 - pY1)^(1 - Y))^S *
      (pY0^Y * (1 - pY0)^(1 - Y))^(1 - S)
  }

  log(sum(weights * integrand(nodes)))
}

# Full log-likelihood
ll <- function(pars) -sum(mapply(ll_i, S=S, Y=Y,
                                  MoreArgs=list(pars=pars, X=X, Z=Z)))

# Optimise
fit <- optim(par=rep(0, npars), fn=ll, method="BFGS",
             control=list(maxit=2000))
```

### Treatment effect computation

```r
# ATE(x) at the sample mean of X
ate_x <- function(beta1, beta0, a1, a0, x) {
  pnorm(x %*% beta1 / sqrt(1 + a1^2)) -
  pnorm(x %*% beta0 / sqrt(1 + a0^2))
}

# MTE(x, u_S) — numerically via quadrature
mte_x <- function(beta1, beta0, a1, a0, x, uS) {
  integrand <- function(theta) {
    (pnorm(x %*% beta1 + a1*theta) -
     pnorm(x %*% beta0 + a0*theta)) *
     dnorm(uS + theta)
  }
  sum(weights * integrand(nodes)) / (dnorm(uS) / sqrt(2))
}
```

### Bootstrap standard errors

```r
B <- 500
boot_ate <- numeric(B)
set.seed(42)
for (b in seq_len(B)) {
  idx    <- sample(nrow(data), replace = TRUE)
  d_boot <- data[idx, ]
  fit_b  <- optim(fit$par, fn = function(p) ll(p, d_boot), ...)
  boot_ate[b] <- ate_x(fit_b$par, x_bar)
}
se_ate <- sd(boot_ate)
```
